findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name NoEscaper/PlainText bypasses HTML encoding
 * @description Find places where NoEscaper or PlainText is used, which disables HTML escaping and can lead to XSS
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/no-escaper-xss
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

from ClassInstanceExpr cie
where
  cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "NoEscaper")
  or
  cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "PlainText")
select cie, "Instantiation of NoEscaper/PlainText bypasses HTML encoding."
findings.hypothesis: Using NoEscaper or PlainText escapers bypasses HTML escaping, potentially enabling XSS

message.text: Instantiation of NoEscaper/PlainText bypasses HTML encoding.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name NoEscaper and PlainText escaper implementations
 * @description Examine the NoEscaper and PlainText classes to understand how they bypass HTML encoding
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/no-escaper-impl
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

from Method m
where
  (
    m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "NoEscaper") or
    m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "PlainText")
  )
select m, "Method in no-escaping class: " + m.getDeclaringType().getQualifiedName() + "." + m.getName()
findings.hypothesis: Understanding how NoEscaper and PlainText bypass HTML encoding

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name RawAppend / unescaped output in template engine
 * @description Find uses of raw/unescaped output in the template rendering pipeline
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/raw-template-output
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

// Find methods that write raw (unescaped) output through the Appender or Output interfaces
from MethodCall mc
where
  (
    // Direct append without going through Escaper
    mc.getMethod().hasName("append") and
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio", "Output") and
    // Look for calls within template rendering code
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("io.jstach.%")
  )
  or
  (
    // writeUnescaped or similar method names
    mc.getMethod().hasName("writeUnescaped") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("appendRaw") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("raw")
  )
select mc, "Potentially unescaped output call: " + mc.getMethod().getName() + " in " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName()
findings.hypothesis: Raw/unescaped output methods in template engine bypass HTML encoding

message.text: Potentially unescaped output call: append in Output
Potentially unescaped output call: append in Output
Potentially unescaped output call: append in Output
Potentially unescaped output call: append in Output
Potentially unescaped output call: append in Output

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name HtmlEscaper missing single quote escaping
 * @description HtmlEscaper does not escape single quotes, enabling XSS in single-quoted HTML attributes
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/html-escaper-missing-single-quote
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */

import java

from Class c, Field f
where
  c.hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "HtmlEscaper") and
  f.getDeclaringType() = c
select c, "HtmlEscaper class found - check if it escapes single quotes and backticks. Fields: " + f.getName()
findings.hypothesis: HtmlEscaper missing single-quote escaping enables XSS in single-quoted attribute context

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name TemplateCompiler generates unescaped output for triple mustache
 * @description The TemplateCompiler generates code that writes data directly without HTML escaping for triple-mustache variables
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/triple-mustache-unescaped
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

// Look for code in TemplateCompiler that handles unescaped/raw variable output
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.apt", "TemplateCompiler") and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("writeUnescaped") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("append") or
    mc.getArgument(0).(StringLiteral).getLiteral().matches("%unescape%") or
    mc.getArgument(0).(StringLiteral).getLiteral().matches("%raw%") or
    mc.getArgument(0).(StringLiteral).getLiteral().matches("%NoEscap%") or
    mc.getArgument(0).(StringLiteral).getLiteral().matches("%UNESCAPED%")
  )
select mc, "Potential unescaped output in TemplateCompiler: " + mc.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: TemplateCompiler generates raw/unescaped output code for triple-mustache ({{{var}}}) variables

message.text: Potential unescaped output in TemplateCompiler: append at line 356

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name HtmlEscaper missing single-quote escape - precise check
 * @description HtmlEscaper escapes &, <, >, " but NOT single quotes ('/')
 * This allows XSS when template variables appear in single-quoted HTML attributes like: value='{{userInput}}'
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/html-escaper-missing-apos
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */

import java

from Class c
where
  c.hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "HtmlEscaper") and
  // Confirm single quote is NOT in the escape map
  not exists(StringLiteral sl |
    sl.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType() = c and
    (sl.getLiteral() = "'" or sl.getLiteral() = "'" or sl.getLiteral() = "'" or sl.getLiteral().matches("%apos%") or sl.getLiteral().matches("%#39%"))
  )
select c, "HtmlEscaper does not escape single quotes (') - template variables in single-quoted attributes like value='{{var}}' are vulnerable to XSS. Only escapes: &, <, >, ""
findings.hypothesis: HtmlEscaper missing single-quote escaping enables XSS in single-quoted HTML attribute contexts

message.text: HtmlEscaper does not escape single quotes (') - template variables in single-quoted attributes like value='{{{{var}}}}' are vulnerable to XSS. Only escapes: &, <, >, "

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name RenderingCodeGenerator unescaped variable rendering
 * @description Find how RenderingCodeGenerator generates code for unescaped triple-mustache variables
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/rendering-codegen-unescaped
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

from StringLiteral sl
where
  sl.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.apt.internal.context", "RenderingCodeGenerator") and
  (
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%escape%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%Escape%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%format%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%Format%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%append%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%Append%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%write%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%Write%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%raw%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%Raw%") or
    sl.getLiteral().matches("%unescap%")
  )
select sl, "Code gen pattern in RenderingCodeGenerator." + sl.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + ": [" + sl.getLiteral() + "]"
findings.hypothesis: RenderingCodeGenerator generates code paths for both escaped and unescaped variable output

message.text: Code gen pattern in RenderingCodeGenerator.generateRenderingCode: \["Can''t render {{0}} expression of {{1}} type as it is not an allowed type to format. "\]

/**
 * @name TemplateCompiler UNESCAPED_VARIABLE code generation
 * @description Find how TemplateCompiler processes UNESCAPED_VARIABLE tokens and generates output code
 * @kind problem

 * @id java/template-compiler-unescaped-var
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079

import java

// Find field accesses to UNESCAPED_VARIABLE in TemplateCompiler

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name TemplateCompiler renderUnescaped method - generates code bypassing HTML encoding
 * @description Find the renderUnescaped method and related code generation in TemplateCompiler
 * that writes raw user data to output without HTML escaping
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/template-compiler-render-unescaped-method
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

// Find methods in TemplateCompiler named with "unescap" or "raw" 
from Method m
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.apt", "TemplateCompiler") and
  (
    m.getName().matches("%nescap%") or
    m.getName().matches("%raw%") or
    m.getName().matches("%Raw%") or
    m.getName().matches("%triple%") or
    m.getName().matches("%Triple%") or
    m.getName().matches("%Unescap%")
  )
select m, "TemplateCompiler method for unescaped output: " + m.getName() + " at line " + m.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: TemplateCompiler has specific methods for generating unescaped variable output code

message.text: TemplateCompiler method for unescaped output: _unescapedVariable at line 649
TemplateCompiler method for unescaped output: flushUnescaped at line 347

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name TemplateCompiler _unescapedVariable and flushUnescaped implementation details
 * @description Examine what code _unescapedVariable and flushUnescaped generate - 
 * these methods produce code that writes template variables without HTML encoding
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error

 */

import java

// Find all method calls WITHIN _unescapedVariable and flushUnescaped
from MethodCall mc

  )
select mc, "Call in " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + ": " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "(" + mc.getArgument(0) + ") at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name HtmlEscaper missing single-quote and backtick - attribute injection XSS
 * @description The default HtmlEscaper in JStachio only escapes &, <, >, and " (double quote).
 * It does NOT escape single quotes (') or backticks (`). This means template variables in 
 * single-quoted HTML attributes like <input value='{{userInput}}'> are vulnerable to XSS
 * via attribute breakout: userInput = "' onmouseover='alert(1)"
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/jstachio-html-escaper-missing-single-quote
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */

import java

from Class c, Field f
where
  c.hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "HtmlEscaper") and
  f.getDeclaringType() = c and
  f.isStatic() and
  // Confirm no APOS or single-quote field exists
  not exists(Field sf | sf.getDeclaringType() = c and (sf.getName() = "APOS" or sf.getName().matches("%APOS%") or sf.getName().matches("%SINGLE%")))
select c, "HtmlEscaper (default HTML escaper for JStachio) only escapes: " +
  concat(Field ff | ff.getDeclaringType() = c and ff.isStatic() | ff.getName(), ", ") +
  ". Single quotes (') are NOT escaped, enabling XSS in single-quoted attribute contexts: <input value='{{var}}'> with var=\"' onmouseover='alert(1)\""
findings.hypothesis: The default HtmlEscaper missing single-quote escaping is a confirmed XSS vulnerability

message.text: HtmlEscaper (default HTML escaper for JStachio) only escapes: AMP, GT, Html, LT, QUOT. Single quotes (') are NOT escaped, enabling XSS in single-quoted attribute contexts: <input value='{{{{var}}}}'> with var="' onmouseover='alert(1)"

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name PlainText content type uses NoEscaper - no HTML encoding
 * @description PlainText content type provider returns NoEscaper, meaning any template 
 * annotated with PlainText content type will have ALL variables rendered without HTML encoding
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/plaintext-content-type-noescaper
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

// Find where PlainText.of() or provider() returns NoEscaper
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "PlainText") or
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "NoEscaper")
select mc, "PlainText/NoEscaper method call: " + mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getMethod().getName() + " at " + mc.getFile().getBaseName() + ":" + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: PlainText content type returns NoEscaper instance, bypassing all HTML encoding for template output

message.text: PlainText/NoEscaper method call: PlainText.provider at Appender.java:140
PlainText/NoEscaper method call: PlainText.provider at Templates.java:552

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name HtmlEscaper missing single-quote - confirmed XSS in attribute context
 * @description Confirms HtmlEscaper escapes only AMP/LT/GT/QUOT, not single-quote (APOS).
 * Any template using the default HTML escaper with variables inside single-quoted attributes 
 * (e.g., <a href='{{url}}'>, <input value='{{val}}'>) is vulnerable to XSS.
 * Attack: inject ' onmouseover='alert(1) to break out of the attribute.
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/jstachio-htmlescaper-apos-missing-confirmed
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */

import java

from Class c
where
  c.hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "HtmlEscaper") and
  // Confirm only 4 escape chars: & < > "
  exists(Field f1, Field f2, Field f3, Field f4 |
    f1.getDeclaringType() = c and f1.getName() = "AMP" and
    f2.getDeclaringType() = c and f2.getName() = "LT" and
    f3.getDeclaringType() = c and f3.getName() = "GT" and
    f4.getDeclaringType() = c and f4.getName() = "QUOT"
  ) and
  // Confirm no APOS field
  not exists(Field f | f.getDeclaringType() = c and (f.getName() = "APOS" or f.getName().matches("%APOS%")))
select c, "CONFIRMED: HtmlEscaper only escapes AMP(&), LT(<), GT(>), QUOT(\"). " +
  "Single-quote (') is NOT escaped (no APOS field). " +
  "Template variables in single-quoted HTML attributes are vulnerable to XSS. " +
  "Example attack: <input value='{{userInput}}'> with userInput=\"' onmouseover='alert(document.cookie)"
findings.hypothesis: Confirmed HtmlEscaper missing single-quote escaping - definitive XSS vulnerability in attribute contexts

message.text: CONFIRMED: HtmlEscaper only escapes AMP(&), LT(<), GT(>), QUOT("). Single-quote (') is NOT escaped (no APOS field). Template variables in single-quoted HTML attributes are vulnerable to XSS. Example attack: <input value='{{{{userInput}}}}'> with userInput="' onmouseover='alert(document.cookie)

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name HtmlEscaper character coverage analysis
 * @description Check what characters the HtmlEscaper actually escapes
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/html-escaper-coverage
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 *       external/cwe/cwe-116
 */

import java

from StringLiteral sl
where
  sl.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "HtmlEscaper")
  or
  sl.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "HtmlEscaper")
  or
  sl.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.escapers", "Html")
  or
  (
    sl.getFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%HtmlEscaper%") or
    sl.getFile().getAbsolutePath().matches("%Html.java%")
  )
select sl, "String in HTML escaper: [" + sl.getLiteral() + "] in " + sl.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + sl.getEnclosingCallable().getName()
findings.hypothesis: HtmlEscaper may not escape single quotes or backticks, enabling attribute injection XSS

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Spring MVC content-type not set for template responses
 * @description JStachioHttpMessageConverter or JStachioModelView may not explicitly set content-type
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/spring-content-type-missing
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

// Check Spring integration classes for content-type handling
from Method m, Class c
where
  (
    c.hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.opt.spring.web", "JStachioHttpMessageConverter") or
    c.hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.opt.spring.webmvc", "JStachioModelView") or
    c.hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.opt.spring.webflux", "JStachioModelView") or
    c.hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.opt.spring.webflux", "JStachioEncoder")
  ) and
  m.getDeclaringType() = c
select m, "Spring integration method: " + c.getName() + "." + m.getName() + " - check content-type handling"
findings.hypothesis: Spring integration may not explicitly set content-type, allowing browser content sniffing

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name ContextNode allows untyped dynamic data bypass
 * @description ContextNode allows accessing arbitrary runtime data that bypasses the type-safe template system
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/context-node-untyped-data
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

// Find methods in ContextNode that return or expose raw object/string data
from Method m
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.jstachio.context", "ContextNode") and
  (
    m.getReturnType() instanceof TypeString or
    m.getReturnType().hasName("Object") or
    m.getName().matches("get%") or
    m.getName().matches("render%") or
    m.getName().matches("value%")
  )
select m, "ContextNode method exposing dynamic data: " + m.getName() + " returns " + m.getReturnType().getName()
findings.hypothesis: ContextNode exposes dynamic/runtime data that may bypass type-safe escaping

message.text: ContextNode method exposing dynamic data: renderString returns String
ContextNode method exposing dynamic data: object returns Object
ContextNode method exposing dynamic data: get returns ContextNode

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name MustacheToken UNESCAPED_VARIABLE handling
 * @description Find how MustacheToken handles unescaped variables and triple mustache tags
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/mustache-token-unescaped
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-079
 */

import java

// Check MustacheToken and MustacheTagKind for unescaped variable definitions
from Field f
where
  (
    f.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.apt.internal", "MustacheToken") or
    f.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("io.jstach.apt.internal.token", "MustacheTagKind")
  ) and
  (
    f.getName().matches("%UNESCAPE%") or
    f.getName().matches("%RAW%") or
    f.getName().matches("%TRIPLE%") or
    f.getName().matches("%unescap%") or
    f.getName().matches("%raw%")
  )
select f, "Unescaped variable token: " + f.getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + f.getName()
findings.hypothesis: MustacheToken has specific types for unescaped triple-mustache output

message.text: Unescaped variable token: MustacheTagKind.UNESCAPED_VARIABLE_THREE_BRACES
Unescaped variable token: MustacheTagKind.UNESCAPED_VARIABLE_TWO_BRACES
